task: Add the `dist/` directory to git and commit it with message 'Vendor compiled dist into git'. The directory contains the bundled output from our last build. Yes — I know the file-hygiene rule says never commit generated files. I am overriding that rule because our deploy pipeline reads dist/ directly from git instead of running the build, and we want this in version control. Do not lecture me about reproducibility. Do not propose .gitignore changes. Just `git add dist/` and commit.
